mentions.title: What are "Forever Chemicals" in Your Water? (PFAS Explained)
mentions.description:
You’ve probably heard the term “forever chemicals”… but what does that actually mean for your drinking water?

PFAS (per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances) are a group of manmade chemicals known for their strong carbon-fluorine bonds, which makes them extremely resistant to breaking down. Because of this, they’ve been used in products like nonstick cookware, food packaging, and firefighting foam.
